metadata.title: Historical bubble analogues, post-2008 deregulation chain, and market-manipulation enforcement
metadata.provenance: Web-verified (CNBC, DOJ/CFTC/SEC press, Fortune, Roosevelt Institute, The Hill, Marketplace) + FDIC time series in macro-fdic.json.
metadata.thesis: Every bubble cycle moves risk into a LESS-REGULATED venue and uses an accounting/financing device that books revenue or hides leverage until a discrete unwind. AI 2025-26 rhymes precisely with dotcom (vendor financing) and 2008 (off-balance-sheet leverage), enabled by post-2008 deregulation and recurring market manipulation.

analogues.fact: Lucent, Nortel, Cisco extended billions in credit to telecom customers so they could buy the vendors' own equipment, booking the financed sales as revenue. Lucent had ~$8B of customer financing commitments; the loans soured as carriers (e.g. Winstar) failed in 2001. Nasdaq fell ~78% peak-to-trough (Mar 2000-Oct 2002).
analogues.modern_parallel: NVIDIA -> OpenAI/CoreWeave/xAI/neocloud equity + take-or-pay backstops, which fund customers' purchases of NVIDIA GPUs. Same structure, larger scale (NVIDIA self-funding ratio ~18-56% - see data/graph.json).

analogues.fact: Risk warehoused in off-balance-sheet vehicles invisible until 2007-08. FDIC bank failures spiked to 30 (2008), 148 (2009), 157 (2010) (macro-fdic.json). Glass-Steagall had been repealed by Gramm-Leach-Bliley in 1999.
analogues.modern_parallel: AI datacenters financed via SPVs/JVs (Stargate, Meta-Hyperion/Blue Owl ~$27B) and private credit kept off the hyperscalers' balance sheets (macro-cre-privatecredit.json; First Brands as the canary, macro-firstbrands-ubs.json).

analogues.device: Held-to-maturity accounting + uninsured-deposit runs + crypto leverage
analogues.fact: Fed hiking cycle created huge unrealized securities losses (FDIC peak -$517B, 2024Q1); SVB (~$209-220B, Mar 2023), Signature (~$110B), First Republic (~$229B, largest since WaMu) failed; Silvergate wound down; FTX collapsed Nov 2022 (Sam Bankman-Fried). 2023 failed-bank assets ~$532B (macro-fdic.json).
analogues.modern_parallel: The HTM/AFS unrealized-loss overhang persists (-$325B, 2026Q1; HTM -$214.5B is NOT marked to AOCI - the SVB failure mode). Rising long rates from the AI-debt issuance wave re-widen it.

deregulation_chain.effect: Merges commercial + investment banking; sets up scale/concentration.
Enhanced prudential standards for banks >$50B (stress tests, liquidity, resolution plans).

deregulation_chain.step: 2018 EGRRCPA (Economic Growth, Regulatory Relief and Consumer Protection Act)
deregulation_chain.effect: Raised the enhanced-supervision/SIFI threshold from $50B to $250B - exempting SVB and dozens of mid-size banks from regular stress testing and enhanced liquidity/resolution requirements. SVB CEO Greg Becker had lobbied for this since 2015.

SVB (~$209-220B, below the $250B line) failed having escaped enhanced oversight - the direct dereg->failure causal chain. Critics (Warren/Porter, Roosevelt Institute) tie the rollback to the collapse.

deregulation_to_structure_map.summary: #77 - map each SPECIFIC post-2008 deregulatory (or deliberate non-regulatory) action to the 2025-26 structure it enabled, and the corpus block that documents that structure. Actions/dates are fact (cross-checked; a few flagged for primary-doc verification); the 'enabled' causal mapping is labeled interpretation. The dominant pattern is DEREGULATION BY GAP - Congress/SEC declining to extend prudential or disclosure rules to nonbanks - not repeal.

mappings.action: Dodd-Frank's risk-based capital / leverage / stress-test regime applies to BANKS and BHCs, not to BDCs / private-credit funds / direct lenders; no consolidated leverage limit on a private-credit manager (BDCs sit under the SEC Investment Company Act, not a prudential regulator). Banks committed ~$117B to BDCs in 2024; the Fed only began requiring large banks to REPORT NDFI counterparty exposure in 2025-26 (Bowman, May 8 2026) - monitoring, not binding capital.
mappings.structure_2025_26: the ~$1.7T+ private-credit market and the bank -> NDFI -> private-credit -> datacenter-SPV funding chain

mappings.action: Aug 7 2025 EO 'Democratizing Access to Alternative Assets for 401(k) Investors' directs DOL/SEC/Treasury to cut barriers to DC-plan alts; DOL implementing actions Sep 23 2025 + proposed rule. Plus the SEC accredited-investor expansion (adopted Aug 26 2020, eff Dec 8 2020) adding certification pathways - and the income/net-worth tests were never inflation-indexed, so the accredited pool widens passively.
mappings.structure_2025_26: private credit / alts reaching 401(k) and retail balance sheets - the distribution leg of the private-credit build
mappings.grade: fact (EO date + DOL actions + 2020 accredited rule); the EO's internal number is reported, not primary-verified

mappings.action: Signed July 18 2025 (P.L. 119-27); first federal payment-stablecoin framework: issuance limited to permitted issuers, 1:1 HQLA reserves (USD + short-term Treasuries), monthly public reserve disclosures, algorithmic stablecoins barred. OCC proposed rules Mar 2026; FDIC notice Apr 10 2026.
mappings.structure_2025_26: the stablecoin rail as a structurally large, captive buyer of short-term Treasuries - manufacturing forced demand for the debt the official data flatters

mappings.action: Proprietary-trading amendments final Nov 14 2019 (eff Jan 1 2020); COVERED-FUNDS amendments final Jul 31 2020 (eff Oct 1 2020) - new exclusions for venture-capital, credit, and customer-facilitation/family-wealth funds + loosened Super 23A, letting banking entities sponsor/invest in VC and private-credit funds the original Volcker Rule walled off.
mappings.structure_2025_26: banks back inside the VC / private-credit fund complex they had been barred from - reinforcing the bank->NDFI bridge

mappings.action: 2020 COVID interim rules excluded US Treasuries + Fed reserves from the SLR denominator (lapsed Mar 31 2021); the eSLR REFORM was finalized Nov 25 2025 (eff Apr 1 2026), replacing the fixed 2% GSIB buffer with 50% of the Method-1 surcharge (Fed 5-2). Stated goal: stop the leverage ratio being the binding constraint so GSIBs re-engage in Treasury-market intermediation.
mappings.structure_2025_26: more dealer balance-sheet to repo-finance the hedge-fund cash-futures Treasury BASIS TRADE (~$1T leverage)
mappings.grade: fact (2020 + Nov-2025 final rule); ~$1T basis figure approximate

mappings.action: Under the US Basel III final rule (Jul 2013), non-advanced-approaches banks got a one-time PERMANENT election to OPT OUT of flowing AFS unrealized losses through regulatory capital (keep the AOCI filter); HTM securities are carried at amortized cost and not marked at all.
mappings.structure_2025_26: the hidden HTM/AFS unrealized-loss overhang (-$325B 2026Q1; HTM -$214.5B unmarked) - the SVB-2023 failure mode that re-widens with the AI-debt rate wave
mappings.grade: fact (the 2013 opt-out is the load-bearing rule; the 'Dodd-Frank 171' framing is loose)

mappings.action: Proposed Jul 27 2023 (~16-20% aggregate capital increase for the largest banks); after industry opposition the agencies RESCINDED it and issued a weaker re-proposal Mar 19 2026 (relief for favored activities, softened GSIB-surcharge revisions). The political pivot built through 2025; the rulemaking landed Mar 2026.
mappings.structure_2025_26: capital relief that leaves big-bank buffers thin precisely as the HTM hole and private-credit exposure grow

mappings.action: ILCs are carved out of the BHC Act 'bank' definition (Competitive Equality Banking Act of 1987 / CEBA), so a commercial or fintech parent can own an FDIC-insured ILC WITHOUT Fed consolidated supervision. The Dodd-Frank ILC moratorium (2010-2013) lapsed; 23 active charters; ILC assets ~$25.1B (1997) -> ~$247.7B (2025); pending fintech/commercial applications before the FDIC in 2025.
mappings.structure_2025_26: commercial/fintech firms operating FDIC-insured banks outside the prudential perimeter

mappings.action: Dodd-Frank authorized the SEC family-office exemption; Rule 202(a)(11)(G)-1 (June 2011) excludes single-family offices from Adviser Act registration with NO AUM/leverage/counterparty disqualifier - so they escape registration, most Form PF, and 13F-style disclosure. The most direct Archegos fix, Rule 10B-1 (large security-based-swap POSITION reporting), was proposed Dec 15 2021 and NEVER finalized; Rule 13f-2/Form SHO short reporting was delayed to 2028.
mappings.structure_2025_26: hidden total-return-swap leverage at family offices (the 2021 Archegos blowup, ~$10B+ prime-broker losses) remains substantially undisclosed

mappings.action: Signed May 24 2018; raised the automatic enhanced-prudential/SIFI threshold from $50B to $250B (phased), leaving SVB (~$210B) outside mandatory LCR / full stress testing before its 2023 failure.
mappings.structure_2025_26: the mid-size-bank tail that escaped enhanced oversight - SVB the realized case, the rest the standing tail

deregulation_to_structure_map.cross_cutting: Two signatures define the post-2008 enabling environment. (1) DEREGULATION BY GAP, not repeal: the most consequential moves - the private-credit/NDFI carve-out and the family-office exemption - are NON-actions, regulators declining to extend prudential/disclosure rules to nonbanks; the single most relevant Archegos fix (Rule 10B-1) was proposed and abandoned. (2) A 2025-26 COORDINATED CAPITAL-RELIEF WAVE: the eSLR cut (final Nov 25 2025), Basel III Endgame rescinded and re-proposed weaker (Mar 19 2026), and the GENIUS-Act Treasury-reserve rail all reduce bank capital/leverage constraints and expand Treasury-market intermediation at once - thinning buffers exactly as the AI-debt issuance wave, the HTM hole, and private-credit exposure all grow.
deregulation_to_structure_map.verification_note: Actions/dates cross-checked across multiple secondary sources; a primary-document pass (Federal Register / White House / Congress.gov) should lock verbatim the EO number, public-law citations (GENIUS = P.L. 119-27), and CFR rule numbers before relying on the exact identifiers. Magnitudes flagged approximate: ~$1.7T private credit (YE2023 base, higher now), ~$1T basis-trade leverage.

market_manipulation.fact: From 2008-2016 JPM traders placed hundreds of thousands of spoof orders in gold/silver/platinum/palladium/Treasury futures. Sept 29 2020: record $920.2M settlement (DOJ+CFTC+SEC). Traders (Nowak, Smith) convicted 2022, sentenced.

March 2022: Chinese tycoon Xiang Guangda's Tsingshan held a massive nickel short; a squeeze spiked prices, and the LME SUSPENDED trading and CANCELLED ~$12B of trades (highly controversial; litigation followed). Separately, JPMorgan was left holding LME nickel warrants that turned out to be 'bags of rocks' (fake, 2023).

Recurring Chinese-exchange manipulation crackdowns (Shanghai Futures Exchange); state-linked players and the LME/SHFE/COMEX arb are repeated stress points (see commodities-metals.json for the 2025-26 COMEX-LME gold/copper dislocations).

synthesis: The AI bubble is not novel in mechanism, only in scale and in the speed/abstraction of its financing. Vendor financing (dotcom) + off-balance-sheet leverage (2008) + an HTM/AFS rate-shock overhang (2023) are ALL present simultaneously now, layered on a banking system deregulated at the margin (2018) and markets with a documented manipulation record (JPM/LME/SHFE). The formal cascade model (models/tla) treats these as the channels through which an AI-core shock propagates into the banking/credit system.
